title: biscutella didyma var. apula
intro: Biscutella didyma var. apula is a plant species within the genus Biscutella, commonly known as the 'alpine biscutella'. It is a flowering plant native to the Apennine Mountains in Italy, characterized by its delicate yellow flowers and slender stems. The medicinal properties of Biscutella didyma var. apula include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid content. This plant is used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to support urinary health and alleviate symptoms of kidney stones and urinary tract infections.
